archimate:Assessment.name: Gaia-X Data Spaces Community
archimate:Assessment.documentation:
Definition: The Gaia-X Data Spaces Community is a collaborative initiative that aims to create a secure and trustworthy data infrastructure in Europe. It brings together various stakeholders, including businesses, research institutions, and public entities, to develop and implement standards for data sharing and usage. The community focuses on creating 'data spaces' - secure virtual environments where data can be shared and analyzed while ensuring privacy and compliance with European regulations. These data spaces are designed to foster innovation, enhance competitiveness, and support the digital sovereignty of Europe.
Source: EIRA Team research
Additional information: The Gaia-X Data Spaces Community is a collaborative initiative that aims to create a secure, trustworthy, and federated data infrastructure in Europe. This community is part of the larger Gaia-X project, which is a European endeavor to establish a competitive, secure, and trustworthy data infrastructure for Europe.
The Gaia-X Data Spaces Community is composed of various stakeholders, including businesses, research institutions, public sector organizations, and other interested parties. These stakeholders work together to define the requirements, standards, and rules for data spaces, which are virtual environments where data can be shared and exchanged in a secure and controlled manner.
The community's main goal is to create a framework for data spaces that ensures data sovereignty and data availability. Data sovereignty refers to the idea that data is subject to the laws and governance structures of the country in which it is located. Data availability, on the other hand, refers to the ability to access and use data when and where it is needed.
The Gaia-X Data Spaces Community is organized into several working groups, each focusing on a specific aspect of data spaces. These working groups include the Data Spaces Architecture and Standards Working Group, the Data Spaces Use Cases and Requirements Working Group, and the Data Spaces Governance and Certification Working Group.
The community also collaborates with other Gaia-X communities and working groups, as well as with external organizations and initiatives. This collaboration is crucial for ensuring that the data spaces framework is compatible with other components of the Gaia-X infrastructure and with the broader data ecosystem.
In summary, the Gaia-X Data Spaces Community is a collaborative initiative that aims to create a secure, trustworthy, and federated data infrastructure in Europe. It brings together various stakeholders to define the requirements, standards, and rules for data spaces, with the goal of ensuring data sovereignty and data availability.
Example: The Gaia-X Data Spaces Community is a collaborative initiative aimed at creating a secure and trustworthy data infrastructure in Europe. Here are some examples of the community:
1. Automotive Data Space: This community focuses on the automotive industry, working on solutions for secure data exchange between manufacturers, suppliers, and service providers. They might work on projects like connected cars, autonomous driving, and predictive maintenance.
2. Health Data Space: This community works on solutions for secure and efficient data exchange in the healthcare sector. They might work on projects like electronic health records, telemedicine, and personalized medicine.
3. Agriculture Data Space: This community focuses on the agricultural sector, working on solutions for secure data exchange between farmers, suppliers, and researchers. They might work on projects like precision farming, crop yield prediction, and sustainable farming practices.
4. Energy Data Space: This community works on solutions for secure and efficient data exchange in the energy sector. They might work on projects like smart grids, renewable energy forecasting, and energy efficiency.
5. Public Sector Data Space: This community focuses on the public sector, working on solutions for secure data exchange between government agencies, citizens, and businesses. They might work on projects like e-government, open data, and digital public services.
6. Financial Services Data Space: This community works on solutions for secure and efficient data exchange in the financial services sector. They might work on projects like open banking, fraud detection, and risk management.
7. Manufacturing Data Space: This community focuses on the manufacturing sector, working on solutions for secure data exchange between manufacturers, suppliers, and customers. They might work on projects like Industry 4.0, predictive maintenance, and supply chain optimization.
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/Gaia-XDataSpacesCommunityAssessment
ABB name: egovera:Gaia-XDataSpacesCommunityAssessment
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/Gaia-XDataSpacesCommunityAssessment
Name: Gaia-X Data Spaces Community Assessment
Interoperability Layer: N/A

properties.value: The Gaia-X Data Spaces Community is a collaborative initiative that aims to create a secure and trustworthy data infrastructure in Europe. It brings together various stakeholders, including businesses, research institutions, and public entities, to develop and implement standards for data sharing and usage. The community focuses on creating 'data spaces' - secure virtual environments where data can be shared and analyzed while ensuring privacy and compliance with European regulations. These data spaces are designed to foster innovation, enhance competitiveness, and support the digital sovereignty of Europe.

The Gaia-X Data Spaces Community is a collaborative initiative aimed at creating a secure and trustworthy data infrastructure in Europe. Here are some examples of the community:
1. Automotive Data Space: This community focuses on the automotive industry, working on solutions for secure data exchange between manufacturers, suppliers, and service providers. They might work on projects like connected cars, autonomous driving, and predictive maintenance.
2. Health Data Space: This community works on solutions for secure and efficient data exchange in the healthcare sector. They might work on projects like electronic health records, telemedicine, and personalized medicine.
3. Agriculture Data Space: This community focuses on the agricultural sector, working on solutions for secure data exchange between farmers, suppliers, and researchers. They might work on projects like precision farming, crop yield prediction, and sustainable farming practices.
4. Energy Data Space: This community works on solutions for secure and efficient data exchange in the energy sector. They might work on projects like smart grids, renewable energy forecasting, and energy efficiency.
5. Public Sector Data Space: This community focuses on the public sector, working on solutions for secure data exchange between government agencies, citizens, and businesses. They might work on projects like e-government, open data, and digital public services.
6. Financial Services Data Space: This community works on solutions for secure and efficient data exchange in the financial services sector. They might work on projects like open banking, fraud detection, and risk management.
7. Manufacturing Data Space: This community focuses on the manufacturing sector, working on solutions for secure data exchange between manufacturers, suppliers, and customers. They might work on projects like Industry 4.0, predictive maintenance, and supply chain optimization.

The Gaia-X Data Spaces Community is a collaborative initiative that aims to create a secure, trustworthy, and federated data infrastructure in Europe. This community is part of the larger Gaia-X project, which is a European endeavor to establish a competitive, secure, and trustworthy data infrastructure for Europe.
The Gaia-X Data Spaces Community is composed of various stakeholders, including businesses, research institutions, public sector organizations, and other interested parties. These stakeholders work together to define the requirements, standards, and rules for data spaces, which are virtual environments where data can be shared and exchanged in a secure and controlled manner.
The community's main goal is to create a framework for data spaces that ensures data sovereignty and data availability. Data sovereignty refers to the idea that data is subject to the laws and governance structures of the country in which it is located. Data availability, on the other hand, refers to the ability to access and use data when and where it is needed.
The Gaia-X Data Spaces Community is organized into several working groups, each focusing on a specific aspect of data spaces. These working groups include the Data Spaces Architecture and Standards Working Group, the Data Spaces Use Cases and Requirements Working Group, and the Data Spaces Governance and Certification Working Group.
The community also collaborates with other Gaia-X communities and working groups, as well as with external organizations and initiatives. This collaboration is crucial for ensuring that the data spaces framework is compatible with other components of the Gaia-X infrastructure and with the broader data ecosystem.
In summary, the Gaia-X Data Spaces Community is a collaborative initiative that aims to create a secure, trustworthy, and federated data infrastructure in Europe. It brings together various stakeholders to define the requirements, standards, and rules for data spaces, with the goal of ensuring data sovereignty and data availability.
